title: ficus sarmentosa var. henryi
intro: Ficus sarmentosa var. henryi, also known as Tie Guan Yin or Iron Plow, is a variety of climbing fig native to China, characterized by its aerial roots and small, oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ficus sarmentosa var. henryi include reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Ficus sarmentosa var. henryi is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine formulas to promote overall well-being.
